# Alluvial Fan

**Summary**: A cone-shaped deposit of sediment formed by the action of water and gravity.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] characterized alluvial fan deposits in the proximal region.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] Identifying high-energy, proximal sedimentary systems.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] Identified via lithofacies analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Alluvial fan facies (FA1) were primarily deposited in meter-scale channel-bar systems with high energy flow conditions. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 107]])
